title: Missouri Revised Statutes § 362.463 - Out-of-state bank may relocate to this state
text:
362.463.  Out-of-state bank may relocate to this state. — An out-of-state bank may, with the approval of the director of the division of finance, relocate its main banking house up to thirty miles to a location in Missouri.  The director shall grant a certificate of incorporation to any out-of-state bank which has relocated its main office to Missouri pursuant to the provisions of this section.
(L. 1995 H.B. 63, et al. § 8) 
Effective 6-13-95
